page: Microsoft Azure D48as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The configuration provides 48 vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 393 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests all scoring in the top 25%. Single-core performance is also strong in stress-ng, though Geekbench single-core results fall into the middle 50%. Memory performance is a notable highlight, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10%, despite standard memory bandwidth tests registering in the average range. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing benchmarks also show strong top-25% performance, while database operations, static web serving, and software compilation remain in the average tier.

The hardware profile reveals specific tradeoffs, particularly in large-scale computational tasks. While text generation using a small LLM model is strong, text generation with a large 70B model falls into the bottom 25%, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, strong multi-core processing, and premium storage capabilities, the D48as_v4 is suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, multi-threaded data processing, image processing, and standard database operations.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv4 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating at 3.525 GHz, it delivers 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM and 393 GB of local storage. The platform supports Premium Storage but lacks GPU resources. Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory scores, alongside average memory bandwidth and database throughput. While multi-threaded tasks like bzip3 decompression and image processing perform in the top 25%, large-scale LLM text generation is weak due to the absence of hardware accelerators. This configuration is suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, multi-threaded data processing, and standard database workloads requiring a balanced resource profile.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 192 GB RAM, and 393 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 393 GB local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, strong multi-core performance, and premium storage capabilities.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 48 vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 393 GB of local storage
Delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory benchmark scores
Supports Premium Storage for demanding disk I/O requirements
Suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, multi-threaded processing, and database operations
